name: Masa Biscuits
recipeIngredient: 1 Stick (4 oz.) unsalted butter, well chilled, cut into small pieces
1/2 cup Solid vegetable shortening, well chilled, and cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 450°F. Position racks in the upper and middle thirds of the oven.
2. In a large bowl, stir together 3 1/2 cups of flour, masa harina de maiz, baking powder, and salt.
3. Blend in the butter and shortening with a pastry cutter until the mixture resembles a coarse, slightly lumpy dough.
4. Stir in the buttermilk until a soft, crumbly dough is formed.
5. Sprinkle half of the remaining flour on a work surface. Turn the dough out and gather it into a ball.
6. Knead the dough briefly just until it holds together. Flatten the dough and sprinkle with the rest of the flour.
7. Roll the dough about 1-inch thick. Cut biscuits using a round 3-inch cutter and transfer to ungreased baking sheets, spacing them about 2 inches apart.
8. Gather scraps into a ball, roll to 1-inch thick, and cut out remaining biscuits.
9. Bake on racks in the oven for 15 minutes, exchanging sheet positions from top to bottom and front to back at halfway point, or until golden and crisp.
10. Serve hot or warm. Makes 12 biscuits.
